name: Type Inference
def: The compiler deduces types automatically so they need not be written explicitly.
problem: Keep strong static typing without the verbosity of annotating every value.
mechanism: Local (flow-based) inference propagates from initialisers/usage; full Hindley-Milner solves constraints globally. Mainstream languages mostly do local inference.
limits: Inferred types can obscure intent at API boundaries
Whole-program HM is rare in these langs
failures: Surprising inferred types (e.g. var → wrong overload)
myths: "Inference = dynamic typing" — types are still fully static
